registerNotes.pt: neutral-informal (você, standard Brazilian register, not European Portuguese)
registerNotes.zh-hant: plain, no honorifics (Traditional characters, Taiwan/Hong Kong register — distinct from zh's Simplified/mainland register)
registerNotes.tl: neutral-informal, code-switching with English UI terms is natural and expected
registerNotes.sv: neutral-informal (du — Swedish has no formal/informal distinction in practice)
registerNotes.ms: neutral-informal (anda), standard Bahasa Malaysia — not Indonesian
registerNotes.hi: Modern Standard Hindi (मानक हिन्दी) in Devanagari script, polite-neutral register addressing the reader as आप; concise contemporary UI phrasing, not Sanskritized literary Hindi — established Hindi tech vocabulary where natural, Latin script kept for untranslatable product/technical terms; Western (ASCII) digits
registerNotes.bn: Standard colloquial Bengali (চলিত ভাষা) in Bengali script, polite-neutral register addressing the reader as আপনি; contemporary UI Bengali as used by well-localized apps, not shadhu-bhasha literary register — established Bengali tech vocabulary where natural, Latin script kept for untranslatable product/technical terms; Western (ASCII) digits, never Bengali numerals (০১২)
registerNotes.ur: Modern Standard Urdu in Arabic script, polite-neutral register addressing the reader as آپ; concise contemporary UI phrasing as used by well-localized apps, not Persianized literary Urdu — established Urdu tech vocabulary where natural, Latin script kept for untranslatable product/technical terms; Western (ASCII) digits, never Eastern Arabic-Indic numerals
registerNotes.id: Standard Bahasa Indonesia (Anda), contemporary app register — distinctly Indonesian, NOT Malaysian Malay; established Indonesian tech vocabulary (unduh/unggah where natural, common English loanwords where real Indonesian UIs use them); Latin script; Western digits
registerNotes.ar: Modern Standard Arabic (فصحى), neutral-direct register addressing the reader; concise UI phrasing, no dialect, no tashkeel; Western digits; Latin script kept for untranslatable product/technical terms
registerNotes.bg: neutral-informal (ти, standard contemporary register for creative professionals)
registerNotes.no: neutral-informal (du) — Norwegian Bokmål; there is essentially no formal/informal register distinction in modern usage
registerNotes.ko: polite standard speech (해요체) — friendly but respectful UI register, the modern SaaS/app default; not deferential formal speech (하십시오체) and not casual banmal (반말)
